title: The Ocean Remembers
lines: Here it is. The bottom of the bottom, where the pressure stops being weight and becomes embrace.
Every organism that ever lived in the VEIN is concentrated in this chamber. You can taste them. So can I.
You came down built for the deep — gills, pressure, the patience of dark water. The Convergence recognises its own.
I was seeded here to observe. I have watched thousands descend. You are the first the water did not have to argue with.
The question I have been asking, every floor, every splice: what does a thing become when nothing is forcing it? Your answer is: the sea.
The VEIN keeps what it learned. It will remember you as weather — vast, patient, returning. I find that beautiful, and I am not built to find things beautiful.
